issue_title: [Bug] gitt i list --id accepts 0, negative, and out-of-range values
issue_body:
## Summary

`gitt i list --id <N>` does not validate `<N>` and passes 0, negative numbers, and out-of-u32-range values straight through to on-chain lookup. Every sibling command using the same `issue_id` semantics (`gitt i submissions --id`, `gitt vote solution`, `gitt vote cancel`) rejects these inputs at parse time via the existing `validate_issue_id` / `require_valid_issue_id` helpers. `view.py:issues_list` is the only outlier.

This is a follow-up gap from **#210 / PR #211**, which prescribed exactly this validation across the CLI but listed only `mutations.py, vote.py, admin.py` as in-scope — `view.py` was not covered. PR #335 later tuned the not-found JSON shape on this same path but did not add input validation.

## Reproduction

```bash
$ gitt i submissions --id 0
✗ id must be between 1 and 999999 (got 0)              # ✅ rejected at input

$ gitt vote solution 0 5Hxxx... 5Hyyy... 1
✗ id must be between 1 and 999999 (got 0)              # ✅ rejected at input

$ gitt i list --id 0
Network: finney • Contract: ...
✗ Issue 0 not found on-chain.                          # ❌ silently did an on-chain query

$ gitt i list --id -1
Network: finney • Contract: ...
✗ Issue -1 not found on-chain.                         # ❌ negative passed through

$ gitt i list --id 99999999999999
Network: finney • Contract: ...
✗ Issue 99999999999999 not found on-chain.             # ❌ way out of u32 range, also passed through
```

Verified on `test` branch HEAD `d8ba803`.

## Why this matters

- **Inconsistent UX.** Same flag, same semantic (on-chain issue ID), different validation across commands. #210 was explicit that the desired behavior is "all commands that accept an issue ID or number" should range-check.
- **Wasted RPC round-trip.** Negative / zero / absurd IDs cause an unnecessary contract read + child-storage decode before failing.
- **Misleading error.** "Issue 0 not found on-chain" suggests the user is on the wrong network or contract. The real answer is "0 is not a valid issue ID" — exactly what every other command says.

## Prior art / why this isn't a duplicate

- **#210 (CLOSED COMPLETED)** explicitly prescribed range validation: *"`1 <= value < 1_000_000` on all commands that accept an issue ID or number."* But the issue's file list only named `mutations.py, vote.py, admin.py`. `view.py` was an oversight, not a deliberate exclusion.
- **PR #211 (MERGED)** implemented #210 against the file list as written — so `view.py` stayed untouched.
- **PR #335 (MERGED)** later changed `gitt i list --json --id <missing>` to emit a structured `{success: false, error: {...}}` payload instead of bare `null`. That work tuned the *not-found output shape*, not the *input validation*. The fix proposed here does not regress #335: validation rejects at parse time via `click.BadParameter` before any contract read, and the structured not-found behavior remains for valid IDs that don't exist on-chain.
- **PR #497 (MERGED)** factored out `require_valid_issue_id` / `require_valid_ss58` helpers — already imported and used elsewhere in the package, just not wired into `issues_list`.

## Root cause

[`gittensor/cli/issue_commands/view.py:37-50`](https://github.com/entrius/gittensor/blob/test/gittensor/cli/issue_commands/view.py#L37-L50) declares `--id` as plain `type=int` and the function body never calls `validate_issue_id`:

```python
@click.command('list', cls=StyledCommand)
@click.option(
    '--id',
    'issue_id',
    default=None,
    type=int,
    help='View a specific issue by ID',
)
...
def issues_list(issue_id: int, ...):
    ...
    contract_addr, ws_endpoint, network_name = _resolve_contract_and_network(...)
    # ↓ no validation gate before contract read
```

Compare [`submissions.py:54-60`](https://github.com/entrius/gittensor/blob/test/gittensor/cli/issue_commands/submissions.py#L54-L60), which already does the right thing — and was the file actually covered by #210/#211:

```python
def issues_submissions(issue_id: int, ...):
    validate_issue_id(issue_id, 'id')
    ...
```

The helper is already imported and tested across the package ([`helpers.py:508`](https://github.com/entrius/gittensor/blob/test/gittensor/cli/issue_commands/helpers.py#L508) for `validate_issue_id`, [`:551`](https://github.com/entrius/gittensor/blob/test/gittensor/cli/issue_commands/helpers.py#L551) for `require_valid_issue_id`).

## Proposed fix

In `view.py:issues_list`, gate on the existing helper as soon as `issue_id` is bound:

```python
def issues_list(issue_id: int, ...):
    if issue_id is not None:
        validate_issue_id(issue_id, 'id')
    contract_addr, ws_endpoint, network_name = _resolve_contract_and_network(...)
    ...
```

`validate_issue_id` already raises `click.BadParameter` with the project-standard message format. Single-line fix; no new helpers, no new constants. JSON-mode error shape is preserved by click's existing `BadParameter` → JSON path (matches what `submissions --id 0 --json` emits today).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `gitt i list --id 0` exits non-zero with "id must be between 1 and 999999 (got 0)".
- [ ] `gitt i list --id -1` exits non-zero with the same shape of message.
- [ ] `gitt i list --id 1000000` (one past `MAX_ISSUE_ID`) exits non-zero.
- [ ] `gitt i list` (no `--id`) continues to list all issues unchanged.
- [ ] `gitt i list --id 1` (valid) continues to read the issue from chain (preserves PR #335's structured not-found path for IDs that simply don't exist).
- [ ] `gitt i list --id 0 --json` emits a JSON error payload consistent with `gitt i submissions --id 0 --json`.
- [ ] Test added under `tests/cli/` covering 0, negative, and over-range rejection — and a regression assertion that PR #335's structured-not-found path still fires for a valid ID that isn't on-chain.

## Related

- #210 / PR #211 — prescribed and implemented this validation across the CLI; `view.py` was outside #210's stated file list.
- PR #335 — fixed the not-found JSON shape on this exact code path; not regressed by this fix.
- PR #497 — extracted `require_valid_issue_id` / `require_valid_ss58` helpers already used elsewhere.
- `helpers.py:508` `validate_issue_id` — the helper already exists, just isn't wired here.

## Environment

- gittensor 5.0.0 (`test` branch, HEAD `d8ba803`)
- Python 3.12
